title: Artanthe flagellaris
taxonomy: Artanthe flagellaris belongs to the family Passifloraceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the passionflower family. It is native to South America and is part of the genus Artanthe, which contains a small number of species. The classification of Artanthe flagellaris is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The plant's classification also includes the order Malpighiales and order within family Passifloraceae. As a member of the Passifloraceae family, Artanthe flagellaris shares characteristics with other passionflowers, such as unique flowers and vines.

intro: Artanthe flagellaris, also known as the Flagellate Bitter Stick, is a tropical plant species native to the Asian region, belonging to the family Passifloraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in treating various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Artanthe flagellaris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, among other health concerns.
